mainmatter.p: Let A_ \bullet \xrightarrow { \iota _ \bullet } { \mathopen {} \left \{ A_ \infty \right \} \mathclose {}} be a universal cocone; we must show that \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } \xrightarrow { \mathsf { L } { \iota _ \bullet }} { \mathopen {} \left \{ \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } \right \} \mathclose {}} is universal as well, i.e. show any cocone \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } \xrightarrow {d_ \bullet } { \mathopen {} \left \{ D \right \} \mathclose {}} factors uniquely through \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } \xrightarrow { \mathsf { L } { \iota _ \bullet }} { \mathopen {} \left \{ \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } \right \} \mathclose {}} in the following sense:

Using the universal property of \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } as a co-comma dcpo, we may define \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } \xrightarrow {d_ \infty }D to be the universal map determined by a certain lax square in the following configuration:

embedded-tex.embedded-tex-body: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare <sq/>{ nw = A_ \infty , sw = A_ \infty , ne = \mathbf {1} _{ } , se = D, north = ! _{ A_ \infty } , east = {h_ \bot }, east/style = {exists,->}, south/style = {exists,->}, south = {h _{ A_ \infty } }, west/style = double, } \node [between = sq/nw and sq/se] {$ \sqsupseteq $}; \end {tikzpicture}

mainmatter.p: First we define { \mathbf {1} _{ } } \xrightarrow {{ h_ \bot }}{ D } to be the element d_i \bot determined by an arbitrary object i \in \mathcal {I}. To see that this is well-defined, fix i,j \in \mathcal {I} to check that d_i \bot =d_j \bot. As \mathcal {I} is connected, we may proceed by induction on a zigzag i \leadsto j \in \mathcal {I}; ultimately, this amounts to checking that for any span i \leftarrow k \rightarrow j in \mathcal {I}, we have d_i \bot =d_k \bot =d_j \bot. This follows from the strictness of lifted morphisms as well as the naturality of the cocone d_ \bullet.
Next we define { A_ \infty } \xrightarrow {{ h _{ A_ \infty } }}{ D } using the universal property of the colimiting cocone { A_ \bullet } \xrightarrow {{ \iota _ \bullet }}{ A_ \infty }:

embedded-tex.embedded-tex-body: \DiagramSquare { nw = A_ \bullet , sw = \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet }, ne = { \mathopen {} \left \{ A_ \infty \right \} \mathclose {}} , se = { \mathopen {} \left \{ D \right \} \mathclose {}} , west/style = embedding, west = \eta _{ A_ \bullet } , north = \iota _ \bullet , south = d_ \bullet , east/style = {exists,->}, east = \exists ! { \mathopen {} \left \{ h _{ A_ \infty } \right \} \mathclose {}} , }

mainmatter.p: To arrange h _{ A_ \infty } and h_ \bot into the desired lax square, we must check that for all a_ \infty :A_ \infty we have h_ \bot \sqsubseteq h _{ A_ \infty } a_ \infty. Fixing arbitrary i \in \mathcal {I} and a \in A_i, it suffices to check that h_ \bot \sqsubseteq h _{ A_ \infty } \iota _{ i } a. As h_ \bot =d_i \bot and h _{ A_ \infty } \iota _ia = d_i \eta _{ A_i } a, our goal follows directly from the monotonicity of { \mathsf { L } {A_i} } \xrightarrow {{ d_i }}{ D }.

p.mark: The reasoning above is not correct, we do not know that we can restrict attention to a \in A_i. This is indeed the case, but need to make it explicit.

mainmatter.p: Thus we have the desired lax square and a unique morphism { \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } } \xrightarrow {{ h }}{ D } factoring it through the co-comma square that defines \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty }:

Our goal is to show that we may choose the universal map { \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } } \xrightarrow {{ d_ \infty }}{ D } corresponding to the cocone { \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } } \xrightarrow {{ d_ \bullet }}{ { \mathopen {} \left \{ D \right \} \mathclose {}} } to be the morphism h defined above. First we must prove that the following triangle commutes:
Using the universal property of the co-comma squares for each \mathsf { L } {A_i}, it suffices to check that two diagrams commute:

li.p: We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting using the strictness of lifted morphisms, this amounts to the established fact that h \bot = h_ \bot.
We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting with established equations, it suffices to observe that the outer diagram below commutes:

ol.p: Finally, we must check that any two morphisms { \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } } \xrightarrow {{ k,k' }}{ D } factoring { \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } } \xrightarrow {{ d_ \bullet }}{ { \mathopen {} \left \{ D \right \} \mathclose {}} } through { \mathsf { L } {A_ \bullet } } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { L } { \iota _ \bullet } }}{ { \mathopen {} \left \{ \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } \right \} \mathclose {}} } are equal. To that end, we will use the universal property of the co-comma square defining \mathsf { L } {A_ \infty } once more to reduce this to checking that k,k' agree on \bot and on \eta _{ A_ \bullet }. The former follows directly from the strictness of \mathsf { L } { \iota _ \bullet }, as we have k \bot = k { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } \iota _ \bullet \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} =d_ \bullet \bot. For the latter, we must check that k \circ \eta _{ A_ \infty } =k' \circ \eta _{ A_ \infty }; by the universal property of the colimiting cocone { A_ \bullet } \xrightarrow {{ \iota _ \bullet }}{ A_ \infty }, it suffices to check that k \circ \eta _{ A_ \infty } \circ \iota _ \bullet = k' \circ \eta _{ A_ \infty } \circ \iota _ \bullet. By naturality of the unit, these are both equal to d_ \bullet \circ \eta _{ A_ \bullet }.

mainmatter.p: Let A_ \bullet be a diagram of dcpos indexed in a connected category \mathcal {I}. Then the lifting endofunctor \mathsf { L } on dcpos preserves the colimit of A_ \bullet.
For any dcpo A, the following is a co-comma square in the posetal 2-category of dcpos, where \mathsf { L } takes a dcpo to its lift or Scott-open partial map classifier.

embedded-tex.embedded-tex-body: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare { nw = A, sw = A, ne = \mathbf {1} _{ } , se = \mathsf { L } {A}, north = ! _{ A } , east = \bot , east/style = embedding, south/style = embedding, south = \eta _A, west/style = double, } \node [between = nw and se] {$ \sqsupseteq $}; \end {tikzpicture}

mainmatter.p: We fix another lax square of the following form:

We must show that there exists a unique morphism \mathsf { L } {A} \xrightarrow {d}D factoring the square above through { \mathopen {} \left \{ d_ \bot \sqsubseteq d_A \right \} \mathclose {}} as follows:

We will define \mathsf { L } {A} \xrightarrow {d}D to take a ^{ ? } : \mathsf { L } {A} to the least upper bound of the directed subset U = { \mathopen {} \left \{ d_ \bot \right \} \mathclose {}} \cup { \mathopen {} \left \{ d_A a \mid a ^{ ? } = \eta _Aa \right \} \mathclose {}} \subseteq D. As this assignment evidently has the correct factorization property, it remains just to check that it is continuous. Letting V be a directed subset of \mathsf { L } {A}, we compute:
For uniqueness, suppose that we have two morphisms \mathsf { L } {A} \xrightarrow {d,d'}D factoring our square in the sense described above and fix a ^{ ? } : \mathsf { L } {A}:
